verses.text: Because Tyre rejoiced that Jerusalem, 'the gates of the people,' was broken, hoping to gain by it, God will bring many nations against her like the sea's waves. Greed exposed. To profit by another's fall is to court God's wrath.

Because Tyre rejoiced that Jerusalem, 'the gates of the people,' was broken, hoping to gain by it, God will bring many nations against her like the sea's waves. Greed exposed. To profit by another's fall is to court God's wrath.

Because Tyre rejoiced that Jerusalem, 'the gates of the people,' was broken, hoping to gain by it, God will bring many nations against her like the sea's waves. Greed exposed. To profit by another's fall is to court God's wrath.
They shall break her walls, scrape her dust, and leave her 'a place for the spreading of nets.' The proud port humbled. The mighty merchant city becomes a bare fishing rock.
They shall break her walls, scrape her dust, and leave her 'a place for the spreading of nets.' The proud port humbled. The mighty merchant city becomes a bare fishing rock.
They shall break her walls, scrape her dust, and leave her 'a place for the spreading of nets.' The proud port humbled. The mighty merchant city becomes a bare fishing rock.
God will bring Nebuchadnezzar, 'a king of kings,' from the north with horses and siege-engines against her. The instrument named. God appoints the very power that will execute His word.
God will bring Nebuchadnezzar, 'a king of kings,' from the north with horses and siege-engines against her. The instrument named. God appoints the very power that will execute His word.
God will bring Nebuchadnezzar, 'a king of kings,' from the north with horses and siege-engines against her. The instrument named. God appoints the very power that will execute His word.
His horses shall cover her in dust; her people slain, her stones and timber cast 'in the midst of the water.' Total ruin. What wealth built, judgment sinks beneath the waves.
His horses shall cover her in dust; her people slain, her stones and timber cast 'in the midst of the water.' Total ruin. What wealth built, judgment sinks beneath the waves.
His horses shall cover her in dust; her people slain, her stones and timber cast 'in the midst of the water.' Total ruin. What wealth built, judgment sinks beneath the waves.
He will silence her songs; she shall be built no more, only 'a place to spread nets upon.' Music stilled. The merriment of a doomed city is forever hushed.
He will silence her songs; she shall be built no more, only 'a place to spread nets upon.' Music stilled. The merriment of a doomed city is forever hushed.
The isles shake at her fall; the sea-princes step down from their thrones, astonished and afraid. A warning to all. The collapse of the great makes the nations tremble.

The isles shake at her fall; the sea-princes step down from their thrones, astonished and afraid. A warning to all. The collapse of the great makes the nations tremble.
When God makes her desolate the deep covers her; He brings her down to the pit, and 'thou shalt be no more.' Erased. The city that ruled the seas is swallowed by them.
